refined_fr.refined_fr: Ensure the NGHE solution achieves or exceeds the customer-defined operational availability target by maintaining system uptime, minimizing downtime, and enabling rapid recovery from faults.
refined_fr.rationale: Availability is a composite metric dependent on system reliability, maintainability, and operational readiness. Decomposing into sub-FRs allows targeted design parameters to address each aspect.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure operational uptime and downtime over the evaluation period; perform fault injection tests and recovery time measurements; compare against customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: Enable rapid fault detection and recovery

candidate_dps: Redundant system components and failover mechanisms
Automated fault detection and recovery algorithms
selected_dp.selected_dp: Redundant system components and failover mechanisms

potential_sources: Interaction between redundancy mechanisms and fault detection algorithms
coupling_check.notes: Redundancy primarily addresses uptime and failover independently; coupling with maintenance and fault detection is manageable with clear interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Redundancy will not introduce excessive complexity that reduces maintainability
Failover mechanisms are reliable and tested under operational conditions
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating predictive maintenance DP in next decomposition level to address downtime minimization.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical components and embedded systems responsible for operational availability, including redundant hardware and failover control.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Send system health and status data to NGHE Solution and Safety Services for monitoring and analysis.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Support communication for failover coordination and remote diagnostics.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of availability-related hardware and embedded control; low diffusion as redundancy logic is localized.
Contains system-level monitoring and coordination but less direct control over redundancy.
Limited containment; mainly interfaces for remote control, some diffusion due to dependency on availability.
Monitors safety alerts related to availability but does not implement redundancy.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Availability-related redundancy and failover mechanisms are best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize diffusion and ensure tight control over physical components.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with NGHE Solution for system-level monitoring may cause interface complexity
Failover coordination across modules may introduce coupling
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts for failover coordination between Vehicle and NGHE Solution to mitigate diffusion risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (redundant components and failover) logically satisfies the refined FR by directly addressing uptime and availability.

diffusion_risks: Failover coordination may require cross-module communication increasing diffusion
Monitoring functions split between Vehicle and NGHE Solution
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module has high containment score for redundancy DP, supporting good architectural packaging.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and fault injection tests at Vehicle module level.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear ownership of availability DP within Vehicle module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface definitions between Vehicle and NGHE Solution for failover coordination
Plan next level FR decomposition to address downtime and recovery explicitly

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement is needed to explicitly address downtime minimization and fault recovery in the FR and DP layers.
next_level_sub_frs: Enable rapid fault detection and recovery

revise.why: To decompose availability into more granular sub-FRs that can be mapped to specific DPs and IMs, improving clarity and coverage.

assumptions: Redundancy mechanisms will be effective in improving availability
Vehicle module can contain redundancy logic with manageable interface complexity
open_risks: Potential diffusion due to failover coordination across modules
Verification complexity for integrated availability metrics
evidence_needed: Operational availability data from prototype or simulation
Fault injection and recovery test results
